frameworks.name: 限制高風險材料
frameworks.desc: X01 Material Restrictions 限制 asbestos、mercury、lead 等已知 hazardous ingredients；例如部分產品類別不可含超過 1,000 ppm asbestos，mercury 不超過 0.1% (1000 ppm) by weight。

frameworks.name: 既有危害與場址風險管理
frameworks.desc: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management、X03 CCA and Lead Management 與 X04 Site Remediation 要處理既有建築 asbestos、lead、PCBs、CCA 與 contaminated sites。

frameworks.name: VOCs 與材料透明
frameworks.desc: X05 Enhanced Material Restrictions、X06 VOC Restrictions、X07 Materials Transparency 與 X08 Materials Optimization 把 product documentation、ingredient transparency、emission thresholds 與第三方評估串在一起。

frameworks.name: 廢棄物、害蟲與清潔政策
frameworks.desc: X09 Waste Management、X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use 與 X11 Cleaning Products and Protocols 對應 hazardous waste、IPM、pesticides、cleaning products 與 maintenance practices。
covers: X10 Pest Management and Pesticide Use

questions.stem: According to Part 3: Restrict Lead of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit for added lead in drinking water pipes, fittings, and solder intended for drinking water distribution and delivery?

options.text: 0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added lead in newly-installed building materials
Products can contain no more than 300 ppm (0.3%) of lead by weight

questions.source_explanation:
It is acceptable for a product to have a weighted wetted average of 0.25% of lead or 
less, that is verified by a third party, or is labeled as ANSI/NSF 372-compliant.

questions.stem: In order to comply with Part 3: Restrict Lead of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit of lead in all newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break switches?

options.text: 0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added lead in newly-installed building materials
Products can contain no more than 100 ppm (0.1%) of lead by weight

questions.source_explanation:
It is acceptable for fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break 
switches to contain no more than 100 ppm (0.1%) of lead by weight.

questions.stem: According to Part 1: Restrict Asbestos of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit of asbestos for acoustic treatments, joint protection, and ceiling product categories?

options.text: 0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added asbestos in newly-installed or applied products within the project boundary
0.25% weighted wetted average for all product categories
0.05% weighted wetted average for all products categories
Product categories cannot contain over 1,000 ppm of asbestos by weight or area

questions.source_explanation:
The following product categories cannot contain over 1,000 ppm of asbestos by 
weight or area: Thermal protection, including all insulation (lagging), applied to pipes, 
fittings, boilers, tanks and ducts, acoustic treatments, sheathing, roofing and siding, 
fire and smoke protection, joint protection, plaster and gypsum board, ceilings, and 
resilient flooring.

questions.stem: To comply with Part 2: Restrict Mercury of Feature X01: Material Restrictions, what is the allowable limit of mercury for newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break switches?

options.text: 0 ppm; there is no acceptable limit for added mercury in newly-installed building materials
Products contain no more than 0.1% (1000 ppm) of mercury by weight
0.05% weighted wetted average for all products categories
0.25% weighted wetted average for all product categories

questions.source_explanation:
Newly installed fire alarms, meters, sensors, relays, thermostats, and load break 
switch products can contain no more than 0.1% (1000ppm) of mercury by weight.

questions.stem: To comply with Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management, a project manager who is overseeing a museum renovation needs to manage exposure risks for asbestos and lead. What other hazardous material must be managed if it is present in the building?
questions.source_explanation:
This WELL feature requires the application of practices to manage exposure risks of 
the hazardous building materials asbestos, lead, and polychlorinated biphenyls 
(PCBs). This is because some materials that were commonly used in construction due 
to their mechanical, chemical, or visual properties have shown to be toxic, and are 
now banned or confined to limited production across the world. However, their 
ubiquitous presence still haunts our built and natural environments and can cause 
disease upon exposure.

questions.stem: To comply with Part 1: Manage Asbestos Hazards of Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management, an investigation of the project space must be conducted by an inspector certified under local regulation or a qualified professional with demonstrable experience where no local regulations apply. At a minimum, what discovery from the investigation must they provide?

options.text: Confirmation of the presence of asbestos is performed through Polarized Light Microscopy (PLM) or Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) testing.
A list of locations where mercury and formaldehyde are located
A building permit waiver from the local municipality for the limited use of asbestos

questions.source_explanation:
A. Confirmation of the presence of asbestos is performed through Polarized Light 
Microscopy (PLM) or Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) testing. 
B. MSDS sheet listing potential hazards 
C. A list of locations where mercury and formaldehyde are located 
D. A building permit waiver from the local municipality for the limited use of asbestos

questions.stem: To comply with Part 1: Manage Pests of Feature X10: Pest Management and Pesticide Use, which of these elements must be included with a management plan for pest control that is based on integrated pest management (IPM) principles?

options.text: Records of pest monitoring data, pest events, pesticide applications, control actions and emergency responses
Records of pest tolerance thresholds with data supporting pesticide application
Pest management flow chart accessible to all occupants
List of identified pests accessible to all occupants

questions.source_explanation: A management plan for pest control must contain the following elements:

questions.stem: According to Part 3: Manage Polychlorinated Biphenyl (PCB) Hazards of Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management, if the caulk is to be disturbed or removed, analysis of the presumably PCB-containing material must follow protocols. How are the protocols mandated?

options.text: By state legislative representatives or by specific US EPA or ISO testing methods
By local laws or, in absence of local laws, by any applicable US EPA or ISO testing methods

questions.source_explanation:
The protocols are mandated by local laws or, in absence of local laws, by any 
applicable US EPA or ISO testing methods.

questions.stem: According to Part 1: Manage Exterior CCA H azards of Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management, if the exterior of a structure was built before laws instituting restrictions on the use of CCA (Chromium Copper Arsenate) and it's found, what can the project team do to comply?

options.text: Provide a verified letter of assurance so wood structures containing CCA can be left in place
Dispose of CCA-containing woods following applicable laws, without incinerating nor wood chipping
Replace all wood structures containing CCA in accordance with the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
Project team can choose to remediate only CCA infused materials that are within the project boundary where human presence is expected

questions.source_explanation:
If CCA (Chromium Copper Arsenate) is found a project team must dispose of 
CCA-containing woods following applicable laws, without incinerating nor wood 
chipping.

questions.stem: According Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management, when is a lead assessment not applicable for a project undergoing WELL v2 Certification??

options.text: Project has accounted for heat island effect.
Project does not have artificial turf.
Project has loose-fill rubber from recycled tires present on playgrounds.
Project has paint peeling on existing playground equipment.

questions.source_explanation:
A project needs to remediate synthetic turf fibers if the total lead concentration of 
synthetic turf fibers is greater than 136 mg/lb, performed by dust-wipe testing per 
EPA, ISO, or locally accepted protocol for dust-wipe testing.

questions.stem: A project has conducted a wipe test for lead loadings of synthetic turf fibers, if found what is the µg/ft2 limit for lead that will make this remediated according to Part 2: Manage Lead Hazards of Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management?

options.text: If the wipe-testing results show lead loadings greater than 40 µg/ft2 replace with turf containing lead concentrations less than 136 mg/kg it must be remediated
If the wipe-testing results show lead loadings greater than 140 µg/ft2 replace with turf containing lead concentrations less than 136 mg/kg it must be remediated
If the wipe-testing results show lead loadings greater than 80 µg/ft2 replace with turf containing lead concentrations less than 300 mg/kg it must be remediated

questions.source_explanation:
If the wipe-testing results show total lead loadings greater than 40 µg/ft2, replace 
with turf containing lead concentrations less than 136 mg/lb.

questions.stem: A WELL project is assessing the paint on existing playground equipment. What percentage of lead, if found, will require the abatement process?
questions.source_explanation: Part 2: Manage Lead Hazards of Feature X03: CCA and Lead Management.

questions.stem: Lead is an existing external hazard that must be identified and remediated on a project. Where may lead be present on a project site?

options.text: Flooring used in a commercial kitchen.
Paints used on outdoor structures and playgrounds.
LED lighting used throughout a project.

questions.stem: The requirement for managing product emissions of newly applied adhesives, sealants, paints, and coatings to meet the California Department of Public Health (CDPH) Standard Method v1.2 can be found in which WELL Feature?

options.text: Feature: X02 Interior Hazardous Materials Management
Feature: X11 Cleaning Products and Protocols

questions.stem: In Feature X06: VOC Restrictions, if a project is in full compliance of both product categories, by Cost or Surface Area, how many points could they achieve?
Which of the following standards establishes limits for the VOC content of furniture for Feature X06: VOC Restrictions?  CDPH  ANSI/BIFMA  SCAQMD  SMACNA

A Fortune 500 company is leasing 5 floors of an existing building. The Business Manager has approved new desks and chairs for 62 employees on one of the floors. The VOC content of at least what percentage (by cost) of the new desks and chairs must meet ANSI/BIFMA e3-2014?
questions.source_explanation:
Part 2: Restrict VOC Emissions from Furniture, Architectural and Interior Products of 
Feature X06: VOC Restrictions.

questions.stem: The presence of pests in indoor environments can have negative health implications for humans. While pesticides can manage insects and rodents, they also bring with them inherent toxicity. What childhood disease have studies shown an increase as a result of pesticide exposure?

options.text: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease in children.

questions.source_explanation: Overview of Feature X10: Pest Management and Pesticide Use.

questions.stem: An integrated pest management plan (IPM) is a way to limit the use of pesticides in and around buildings. If there is any pesticide application within the project, what must be available to occupants and owners?
questions.source_explanation: Feature x10: Pest Management and Pesticide Use.

questions.stem: The phthalates (plasticizers) DEHP, DBP, BBP, DINP, DIDP, and DNOP are limited to 0.01% (100 ppm) in flooring and wall coverings according to which of the following features?

options.text: Feature X02: Interior Hazardous Materials Management

questions.stem: Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions gives 1 point if, at minimum, 50% by cost of newly installed furniture, millwork, and fixtures contain halogenated flame retardants at less than _____?
Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions highlights the concerns of orthophthlates, halogenated flame retardants and ______?

Part 1: Select Compliant Interior Furnishings of Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions sets a standard for textiles and plastics in furniture. We also see a standard set for electrical and electronic products. What must newly installed electrical and electronic products must be compliant to?

Which of the following chemicals found in Textiles and Plastics must be limited to a maximum of 100 ppm in newly installed furniture, millwork, and fixtures, according to Feature X05: Enhanced Material Restrictions?
Which of the following is NOT an acceptable third-party verification that demonstrates Part 3: Select Products with Third-Party Verified Ingredients of Feature X07: Materials Transparency?

options.text: Cradle to Cradle™ Material Health Certificate
